reviews.review_msg: Very solid instructor. Social cognition material was a bit dry but she did her best to make it fun and engaging. If you attend lectures and do the readings, you should do fine. I learned a lot, but didn't do as well I'd hoped.

Sally was great! I'm nearing the end of my semester with her. My only complaint would be how disorganized some aspects of the course were; sometimes she would show up late or cancel class with very little notice. When I asked direct questions, I did not get direct responses; instead, I was merely directed to external links (which were from the TAs)
The best psychology professor I've had at SFU. If you get a chance to take one of her classes definitely do it!

This class did not deserve Dr. Y. Xie. She was so charismatic and kind, but most university students (myself included) just show up because there is a participation mark. I found the course content pretty dull, but she tried her best to make it engaging. Having no tests was refreshing and allowed for some creativity in the projects.

She will quickly be recognized as SFU's finest. Sally is so caring and empathetic to the student experience. She constantly asks for student feedback on the course and is flexible with deadlines. She presents concepts in relatable and interesting ways. No exams, just easy quizzes, participation, few assignments, and a final paper. She's awesome!
